perturbation.original_text: Victorinox shall hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Victorinox shall keep all Confidential Information safe and shall not reveal any Confidential Information to any outside parties.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'hold...in strict confidence' to 'keep...safe' and 'third party' to 'outside parties' introduces ambiguity. The legal interpretation of 'safe' versus 'strict confidence' differ. And 'outside parties' vs 'third party'. This now makes the level of protection needed, less strict. This will now contradict the statement where it needs to take the same degree of care.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Victorinox shall keep all Confidential Information safe and shall not reveal any Confidential Information to any outside parties. Victorinox shall disclose the Confidential Information only to its employees and agents who need to know such information and who are bound by restrictions regarding disclosure and use of such information comparable to and no less restrictive than those set forth herein. Victorinox shall take the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential and proprietary information of similar nature and importance (but in no event less than reasonable care) to protect the confidentiality and avoid the unauthorized use, disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information. The obligations of this Section 2 with respect to any item of Confidential Information shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'hold...in strict confidence' with 'keep...safe' and 'third party' with 'outside parties'. According to the Perturbation Explanation, this introduces ambiguity and makes the level of protection needed less strict, contradicting the contradicted text which requires Victorinox to take 'the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential and proprietary information' and no less than reasonable care. The changed text thus weakens the obligation described in the contradicted text, creating a direct conflict regarding the standard of care required.
